structural_coherence: The 'Course Schedule' section, specifically the 'schedule of topics', is presented as plain text and has lost its original tabular structure (Week | Tasks).

new_boilerplate: Every effort will be made to ensure that grading is as objective and fair as possible. If you believe that there is an error in the grading, please submit, in writing, an appeal within one week of your work being returned. However, please be advised that if you submit such an appeal, the entire assignment/exam will be regraded to ensure that all parts are properly graded. As such, your grade on the exam could increase or decrease based on the secondary grading.
Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).
proposals.reasoning: The proposed false positives are either sub-titles, labels, list items, or table headers that were incorrectly identified as main section headers by the parser. The boilerplate text for 'Grading Appeals' and 'Late Work Policy' are common institutional policies, especially with the reference to 'Student Rule 7', and are likely to appear verbatim across many syllabi. The text cleanups address HTML entities like '<' and ' ' that should be rendered as their corresponding characters or spaces.
